markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Expose graph/backlink/folder/search state through visual-debug snapshots and backend debug payloads. This MT belongs to LoomObsidianNavigation. Deliver the Obsidian replacement surface as a Handshake-native Loom projection.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Obsidian-like behavior must be implemented as Loom/project knowledge projections over PostgreSQL, not a markdown-vault clone.
Backlinks, graph, tags, folders, pins, color labels, and project links must have backend navigation APIs and visual-debug evidence.
scope.expected_behavior: LoomVisualDebugViews leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: LoomVisualDebugViews is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

lifecycle.ready_for_validation_reason: Loom visual-debug backend payload exposes bounded graph, backlink, folder, and search state over PostgreSQL/EventLedger-backed Loom authority; UserManual route coverage, real PostgreSQL proof, cargo check, rustfmt check, and local adversarial review passed.

evidence.summary: Added hsk.loom_visual_debug@1 typed payload, storage trait method, Postgres snapshot projection, GET /workspaces/:workspace_id/loom/visual-debug route, UserManual registry entries, and a real-PostgreSQL proof test target.

Real PostgreSQL MT-191 proof and adjacent regressions passed: loom_visual_debug_views_tests (2 passed), loom_graph_search_filters_tests (3 passed), loom_navigation_fixtures_tests (1 passed), user_manual_api_tests (13 passed), cargo check --features runtime-full,test-utils, targeted rustfmt check, and git diff --check for touched product files.
KB_READY_CHECKLIST_RECEIPT emitted PASS before READY_FOR_VALIDATION transition.

Local read-only adversarial review found no MT-blocking product bug. Checked fail-closed workspace/start/query behavior, limit bounding, trimmed debug payloads, no sidecar/offline authority, UserManual route coverage, and real PostgreSQL proof. A delegated review agent stalled and was closed before returning a verdict, so this evidence records the local review only.

No GUI visual-debug run was performed because this MT delivered the backend debug payload and route; no Loom front-end visual-debug surface exists in scope to inspect. The payload exposes stable route_ids and bounded navigation state for future GUI/model inspection.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-191 requires a bounded backend visual-debug snapshot exposing Loom graph/backlink/folder/search navigation state as a PostgreSQL/EventLedger-backed projection (not a parallel store), with a wired route, UserManual coverage, and real-PG proof. The code delivers exactly that: a real composed Postgres implementation, a GET route registered in the router, matching UserManual registry entries, and a real-PostgreSQL test that fails closed (panics) without a live DB.
findings: storage/postgres.rs:9288 loom_visual_debug_snapshot is a real implementation composing real authority-backed queries (loom_visual_debug_counts SQL COUNT over loom_blocks/loom_edges/loom_folders/loom_folder_members/loom_block_knowledge_bridge at postgres.rs:249, local_graph, get_backlinks_with_context, list_loom_folders, search_loom_graph, loom_folder_member_count). No stub/placeholder.

Snapshot is correctly typed as a projection: storage/loom.rs:836-848 LoomVisualDebugSnapshot carries authority_backend=PostgresEventLedger and authority_class="projection" (postgres.rs:9379-9380), with bounded counts/graph/backlinks/folders/search — not a parallel graph/search store.

Route is wired: api/loom.rs:283-284 registers GET /workspaces/:workspace_id/loom/visual-debug -> loom_visual_debug_snapshot handler (api/loom.rs:3451), which fail-closes on missing start_block_id (HSK-400-LOOM-START-BLOCK-REQUIRED) and missing q (HSK-400-LOOM-QUERY-REQUIRED), clamps limit 1..100, and records a FlightRecorder EventLedger event.

Sub-rule 2 real-resource proof is genuine: tests/loom_visual_debug_views_tests.rs:17-26 pg_or_skip! PANICS (not silent skip) when real PostgreSQL is absent; the test inserts real loom blocks/edges/folder/bridge rows and asserts the snapshot reflects them (counts >=, graph node depth=1, mention edge, backlink context snippet, folder membership sample, graph-search hit, route_ids).

Sub-rule 1: grep of impl + test found no todo!/unimplemented!/placeholder/mock/deferred markers. The trait default at storage/mod.rs:2473 returns StorageError::NotImplemented("loom visual-debug backend") but it is dead/unreachable — Postgres is the only Database backend (no sqlite module in src/storage/) and PostgresDatabase fully overrides the method; the live path never hits the default.

UserManual coverage present and matches test assertions: user_manual/registry.rs:568-573 registers loom.visual_debug (GET /workspaces/:workspace_id/loom/visual-debug, schema hsk.loom_visual_debug@1) and keeps loom.graph_search.
Self-cert: lifecycle.claimed_by=KERNEL_BUILDER-20260614-214032, completed_by=null (READY_FOR_VALIDATION, not self-signed COMPLETED); independent review confirms proof is real.

Aside (non-blocking, expected for winding-down governance): MT-191.json has no lifecycle.implementation_commit; the test/impl landed via a checkpoint commit (c49afb7f) on feat/WP-KERNEL-009. Packet cites v02.192 spec anchors while current is v02.194 — paperwork only, not a code/spec contradiction.
evidence_checked: git log on feat/WP-KERNEL-009 for MT-191/visual_debug commits

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
